summary: Add ruler width editor setting to 2D editor

description.text:
This adds a new editor setting that lets you make the ruler in the 2D editor thicker. The font size adjusts along with the thickness, so if you're using a lower editor scale and you find the ruler numbers illegible, this lets you fix that.

I also made it so that the tick density adjusts with the font size to avoid overcrowding.

90% editor scale with ruler width at the default (15):
![2025-04-03 20_10_19-(_) TestLevel tscn - Gobold - Godot Engine](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1230871-ba00-4d25-89a0-4a35d5bf937d)

90% editor scale with ruler width increased to 22:
![2025-04-03 20_11_44-Editor Settings](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28d9195d-07c9-46c5-9073-877a1e6162a3)

Fixes #104981.

summary: Add desktop notifications for MacOS

description.text:
# Desktop Notifications for macOS

## Summary
Partial implementation for https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/1338. Adds support for desktop notifications on macOS, allowing games and applications to send native system notifications even when the application is not in focus. Also supports scheduling them for future delivery.

## Changes
- Added `OS::send_notification()` method, that can be implemented in any system, with the following signature:
  ```gdscript
  Error send_notification(String title, String message, Callable callback = Callable(), Variant icon = null, int duration = -1, Dictionary datetime_to_send = {})
  ```
- Include MacOS implementation, which includes the following functionalities:
    - Custom Icons
    - Callbacks
    - Scheduling notifications
- Uses `UNUserNotificationCenter` on macOS 10.14+ and falls back to `NSUserNotification` on older versions
- Supports scheduling notifications for future delivery using the following datetime formats:
  - Full datetime: Dictionary with keys "year", "month", "day", "hour", "minute", "second"
  - Unix timestamp: Dictionary with key "unix_time"
  - Relative time: Dictionary with key "relative_seconds"
- Defensively handles edge cases like script mode, headless mode, and invalid app contexts
- Added documentation with examples
- Notifications work for Headless mode, but not for script mode (in which case, notification will be logged, and a warning will be shown).

## Current Limitations
- Notifications will only work when the application is properly exported/bundled (this is a macOS system limitation).
  - If the app is not bundled, notification will at least be logged along with a warning.
- Notifications will only show if application is out of focus.
- The duration parameter is not supported on macOS, as notification duration is controlled by the system's accessibility settings.

## Testing
1. Compile with `scons dev_build=yes generate_bundle=yes`. It will need to be bundled for notifications to work.
2. Download [these scripts](https://gist.github.com/Dowsley/ce2df692c1aa0f6a4a671f49c721bcf0)
3. Run one of the test scripts from the project: `./bin/godot_macos_editor_dev.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot --path /PATH/TO/PROJECT -s test_basic_notification.gd`
    - Each of the 4 scripts is made to test a specific functionality.
    - When you run the script, it will open a window. Since notifications only appear when app is out of focus, they have 2-3 seconds timers for you to click out of the new window to make it lose focus. Otherwise no notification will be shown.
5. If notification does not work even after following steps correctly, you need to go to System Settings > Notifications > GodotTest (or whatever the name of your bundle) > Enable Notifications

### Evidence/Live testing:
#### 1. Basic Notification
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74ff7d81-cb6e-4e27-8014-147ff79f7a6b

#### 2. Icons
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21fadcf1-344d-400e-9daf-528c7b086ea5

#### 3. Callbacks
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2564bea-193a-433f-8194-4c8c2390508b

#### 4. Scheduling (minor correction: it was 10 seconds from the time it was sent, not 15)
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/94176e20-9358-4b81-812e-a2748197c04e

#### 5. Any of the tests above, but executed in an unbundled project
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33d5f0f5-76f9-4347-b94b-6e593744e270)

Also, while you're recording, notifications wont show in MacOS by default. Make sure to change this setting when recording to show me any possible bug.

## What can improve right now
- Did not have an opportunity to test feature on older MacOS versions. Would love some help.

## Future Work
- Investigate a possible implementation for duration.
- Implementation for other platforms, of course.

summary: Cleanup QuickOpenDialog history and fix UID errors

description.text:
The EditorQuickOpenDialog does a check for missing resource paths, but if the path is UID, the check will print an error. This PR prevents that.
I also added a code that will cleanup invalid paths and re-save history when necessary. While normally it does not matter, because history is re-saved when you open something, the dialog wouldn't modify history of a type you didn't open recently. If you had some outdated paths for rarely used types, they'd be loaded and skipped every time for no reason.

* *Bugsquad edit, fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104961*

summary: Jolt/Godot physics inconsistency in detection of `move_and_slide` floor collisions

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Debian GNU/Linux trixie/sid trixie on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7600 (RADV NAVI33) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

With regular Godot physics `move_and_slide`, a `CharacterBody3D` will always report slide collisions (`get_slide_collision_count() > 0`) with a floor, except in the case of a non-constant speed body moving downhill:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6871069-44a3-4fa2-9ed5-03c0827943ad

(red indicates a slide collision is detected)

With Jolt physics, no such slide collisions are reported when moving parallel to a surface.
Additionally, bodies with `constant_speed` inconsistently report slide collisions when not moving, seemingly depending on previous vertical motion.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b07f454-edbc-4775-aea9-6c6f6ba878bf

This is just the out-of-the-box project settings, so I'm not sure if there are Jolt settings that could be tweaked to affect this.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the MRP
2. Use the arrow keys to slide the bodies around

The MRP is a couple of regular capsule `CharacterBody3D`s using the default script template (plus colouration code).  The only difference between the two character bodies is the `constant_speed` flag.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[jolt-surface-collisions.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19586648/jolt-surface-collisions.zip)

summary: Editor Build Profile generation uses wrong names for build options

description.text:
### Tested versions

`4.4.1-stable` (also in current master as of `1f56d96cf2c768c3844c68ccb504dfeee841ae15`)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed, 26 Mar 2025 07:48:57 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) - AMD Ryzen 7 4800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

Generating a custom compilation config from the editor using Project -> Tools -> Engine Compilation Configuration Editor... will write wrong names for some of the build options.

Disabling "3D Physics" results in the following contents in the .build file (truncated to relevant parts):
 ```
{
    "disabled_build_options": {
        ...
        "disable_3d_physics": true,
    }
    ...
}
```

But in the main SConstruct file, the actual check expects the following string: `disable_physics_3d`. (Notice they are different :)). After a short inspection, there are some mismatched options.


| SConstruct  | .build |
| ------------- | ------------- |
| disable_physics_2d | disable_2d_physics |
| disable_physics_3d | disable_3d_physics |
| disable_navigation_2d | disable_navigation | 
| disable_navigation_3d | disable_navigation`(?)` |
| disable_xr | openxr |
| N/A | rendering_device |
| disable_advanced_gui | N/A |
| minizip | N/A |

Edit: Relevant files
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f56d96cf2c768c3844c68ccb504dfeee841ae15/editor/editor_build_profile.cpp#L47
and
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f56d96cf2c768c3844c68ccb504dfeee841ae15/SConstruct#L987

### Steps to reproduce

Generate a custom build configuration containing one the mentioned settings. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Incorrect in-editor value type of AnimationPlayer subproperty track keys (e.g. `Vector2` instead of `float` for `position:x`)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
- v4.4.dev3.official [f4af8201b]
- v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
- v4.5.dev1.official [97241ffea]

### System information

N/A

### Issue description

Since v4.4.dev3 in-editor AnimationPlayer shows incorrect value type for sub-property tracks.
This is a regression from #96789. cc @SaracenOne

Seems to be mainly an UI issue, as the correctly created animation/tracks (using the editor in v4.4.dev2 and earlier, or by manually editing the animation data in `.tscn`) play/animate just fine in v4.4.dev3+.

v4.4.dev2.official [97ef3c837]|v4.4.dev3.official [f4af8201b]
-|-
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08b5fd15-2bb5-45dd-84fc-0f68de4b4a8d)|![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/585385f2-afbd-4ee2-9efb-fd48b2740a82)

Adding a new key, or editing an existing correct-type-value makes the incorrect-type-value be saved. E.g.:
(v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6])
Before adding/changing key|After adding/changing key
-|-
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f5a68cae-848d-4146-ad21-eba8ddad2c07)|![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/284af18d-dac1-4802-82cc-d59b1bdccb1c)![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c3bd3b6-561d-42ed-94be-4306b1f58ac8)

In `.tscn`:
```diff 
@@ -175,10 +175,10 @@ tracks/5/path = NodePath(".:texture:margin:size")
 tracks/5/interp = 1
 tracks/5/loop_wrap = true
 tracks/5/keys = {
-"times": PackedFloat32Array(0, 1),
-"transitions": PackedFloat32Array(1, 1),
+"times": PackedFloat32Array(0, 1, 1.43333),
+"transitions": PackedFloat32Array(1, 1, 1),
 "update": 0,
-"values": [Vector2(0, 0), Vector2(64, 64)]
+"values": [Vector2(0, 0), Rect2(1, 2, 3, 4), Rect2(5, 6, 7, 8)]
 }
```

### Steps to reproduce

Add AnimationPlayer track for a subproperty like e.g. `position:x`, then add a key and try setting the value. In v4.4.dev3 and later it incorrectly shows `Vector2` value type instead of `float` etc.

summary: ERROR: Unrecognized UID: "uid://vfkt4pl67hw3" when opening Quick Open dialog

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.dev1.mono.official [97241ffea]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev1.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3570K CPU @ 3.40GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

When I opened Quick Open... dialog I got these error messages:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ba8857c3-1b0e-40aa-9f7a-b0a8aab04f65)

My first though was that my project is somehow corrupted. When I searched for these unrecognized UIDs are, I found them from `quick_open_dialog_cache.cfg`. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da022bc1-29dd-4a24-aa87-63ad6aeca75a)

So the cache contains files I have already deleted. I think this is a quite normal thing to happen with caches. 

In my opinion no error message should be displayed here. This error message only makes the user to suspect that something is broken. Even displaying the source of the error (quick open dialog cache) would be useless because users do not care about this kind of internal editor things.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Add new gdscript file to a project
2. Open the added gdscript file using Quick Open (`Shift-Alt-O` in Windows)
3. Quit Godot
4. Delete the added gdscript file manually
5. Start Godot and load the project
6. Open Quick Open dialog
==> `Unrecognized UID`

description.text: Also changes project manager dialogs to use equivalent editor settings instead of project settings (which is not accessible to the project manager).

summary: Add quick access to Movie Maker output path in editor bar

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11541 .

This PR adds a button to the editor's run bar next to the Movie Maker button. Clicking the button opens a popup window where the Movie Maker output path can be changed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d691c983-b1a4-4745-a270-282db0f7cff7

To help accomplish this, it also includes a new class, `EditorFilepathSelect`, which consists of a `LineEdit` and a `Button`. Clicking the `Button` opens a file dialog, and choosing a file from the dialog populates the `LineEdit` with its path.

## To Do
- [x] Update the project setting when focus leaves the `LineEdit`
- [ ] Include a checkbox for setting the current scene's movie path instead of the global project path(?)
- [x] Set the `EditorFilepathSelect`'s accepted file types

It isn't necessary for this PR specifically, and would probably increase the amount of time it takes by a lot, so unless it is very desired I won't try to add additional settings to `EditorFilepathSelect` until perhaps a future PR.

summary: Unexpected image buffer size when creating PortableCompressedTexture2D from Image

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.dev [1f56d96cf], 4.4.1.stable [96579d139]

### System information

Windows 11 Vulkan (Forward+)

### Issue description

When trying to create a PortableCompressedTexture2D from an Image, the following error shows when using compressed formats.
```
  ERROR: Expected Image data size of 512x512x3 (RGB8 without mipmaps) = 786432 bytes, got 131072 bytes instead.
  ERROR: Width must be equal or greater than 1 for all textures
```
If `COMPRESSION_MODE_LOSSLESS `is used, there's no error displayed. Most probably, what's happening is that the Image data buffer is compressed here:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f56d96cf2c768c3844c68ccb504dfeee841ae15/scene/resources/portable_compressed_texture.cpp#L182-L204
So the buffer is no longer of the expected size, since it is compressed.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP.
- Open the script `test.gd`
- Then run the script with File > Run

[test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19578958/test.zip)

summary: Mesh materials are not refreshed properly within an active scene when modified externally

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Not reproducibe: v4.4.dev3.mono.official [f4af8201b] and earlier, still fine at least in v4.2.1.stable
- Starts happening from v4.4.dev4.mono.official [36e6207bb]
- Still reproducible in v4.5.dev1.mono.official [97241ffea]


### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12019.1028) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12400 (12 threads)

### Issue description

If a textured mesh is modified externally and the texture removed, while being shown in the active scene, the texture seems to stay loaded in. Similarly, if an untextured mesh is replaced with a textured one, the base color stays as it was before (e.g. red in the given example). The mesh resource thumbnail is also not updated, although that's less of an issue.

I guess some kind of material caching, the commit diff between the versions is massive so I didn't further explore what exactly would be causing it. 

Reopening the scene fixes it, but is very inconvenient when having to do it often as I then have to find where I left off again, especially in large scenes. See the attached video for a demonstration - all the button does is replace the "active.glb" file contents with one of the other selected meshes. 

Expectation: the updated mesh should reflect in the active scene correctly without needing to reopen it

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38f681ca-9d64-480e-9fa9-cb9ec08f4130

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP main.tscn
- Set the "swap mesh" bool
- Move focus out of the editor to trigger a re-import, or manually reimport active.glb

The MRP specifically contains "red.glb" - no texture but with a base color, "artistic.glb" - texture and no base color, and "blue.glb" which is also no texture. Setting the swap meshes to red and blue works as expected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mesh_texture_bug_mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19573601/mesh_texture_bug_mrp.zip)

summary: Exported Typed Dictionaries with Resource values reset at runtime if the Resource uses an _init function.

description.text:
### Tested versions

I encountered this bug in version 4.4. I'm unsure if this is due to the new Typed Dictionaries functionality recently added or if this behavior predates this version.

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-3770K CPU @ 3.50GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

If a custom Resource has an _init function, any exported Dictionary variable that uses that resource will reset at runtime. I don't know if this is only true for typed Dictionaries, nor do I know if it applies to non-export Dictionary variables. But the cause seems to specifically be the Resource in question having an _init function or not. This behavior does not occur if the script has normal functions, just _init as far as my testing goes.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project and a main scene.
2. Add a node to your scene with a script attached that contained an `@export` variable of type Dictionary[String, (custom Resource class)]
3. In the script for the custom resource class, write an _init function
4. create a new key/value pair in the Dictionary variable of the node in your main scene, instantiating the custom resource value
5. Run the project and check the remote tab to see if the Dictionary has any entries or not (It should have reset to be empty)
6. stop the build and comment out the _init function in the custom resource class
7. rerun the project and check if the node's Dictionary still contains the key/value pair including the custom resource (it should)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[dictionaryreseterrordemo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19571839/dictionaryreseterrordemo.zip)

summary: Unique nested resources are indistinguishable from shared resources in Inspector

description.text:
### Tested versions

All

### System information

N/A

### Issue description

There is no visible indication to the user whether a nested resource has been Made Unique.  As a result, users may change a nested shared resource, assuming it is unique, and cause unintended changes elsewhere that the resource is referenced.

Coloring unique resources hot pink when expanded (rather than the purple that is the default theme), and/or changing their icon from a box to a box with an asterisk in it, would go a long way toward reducing accidental errors, and frustration with users who are new to using resources.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a `child.tres` file for custom resource `Child`, then a `parent.tres` file for a custom resource `Parent` who has an `@export var child: Child`.  Point `parent.tres:child` to `child.tres`.

Make Unique `parent.tres:child` in the inspector.

**Expected behavior**:

Visual indication of the Unique status of `parent.tres:child`.

**Actual result**:

No visual indication.  Users must know to hover over the `child` value to see if a .tres appears in the tooltip, or drill down to `parent.tres:child` -> **Resource** group -> `resource_path` and perhaps scroll the text field to the right to determine whether it ends in ".tres" or "::Resource_x1y2z".

summary: GodotPhysics and JoltPhysics calculates auto `center of mass` differently (and no docs about this)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]
- Reproducible in v4.3 in jolt addon

### System information

Windows 10 - Vulkan - Nvidea 1070

### Issue description

It seems GodotPhysics and JoltPhysics calculates the **auto center of mass** in different ways.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/97f5c354-415e-4e26-a92e-41210b1b6d2f)

This description is a bit confusing at first. 
It first says it will "Calculate the center of mass based on its shape.", and then says "Assumes the shape's origin is the center of mass."

It seems this calculates the center of mass when using multiple collision shapes. So this doc is correct for **GodotPhysics**.

But **JoltPhysics** seems to calculate the center of mass of **each shape individually**, which makes this doc wrong for jolt.
A non uniform shape will have its center of mass NOT at the shape's origin.

Not sure what the solution is here.

At the very least, I think we should add this difference in behavior in the [Using jolt physics ](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.4/tutorials/physics/using_jolt_physics.html) notable differences documentation.

JoltPhysics calculating each shape's center of mass while GodotPhysics doesn't can lead to big inconsistencies between the two physic engines.

---
I found this out by change due to a series of other small errors.
I created a simplified convex collision shape based on a BoxMesh, but the resulting shape was kinda off, as I found out later.
This means, the center of mass of this object is not really at the center, so using `apply_force` will rotate the object slightly.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fdc396d9-e10d-435f-b749-4b526e28db08)

But in GodotPhysics, it doesn't. Because center of mass stays at 0,0,0.
While in jolt, it'll rotate the rigidbody slightly. 
And I can see that its center of mass is around (-0.007936, 0.048936, 0) by using the _integrate_forces body state.

This means the same code
```gdscript
func _physics_process(_delta: float) -> void:
  if Input.is_action_pressed("action"):
  	apply_force(Vector3.UP * 25)
```
works very differently between physic engines, and can lead to severe headaches when migrating from one to another in more physic focused games.

Here is in GodotPhysics

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/32f5d243-3844-4e32-99e1-c826a0d3c541

And here is the same in JoltPhysics

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c5215e5-f1a5-49fc-8e38-9439682ba281

--- 
This will only happen if you have irregular shapes, as long as you use primitives shapes that have their center of mass aligned with the origin of the shape, both engines behave the same.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a RigidBody3D
- Create MeshInstance3D child and a BoxMesh sized (2.0, 1.0, 5.0)
- Use the Mesh menu to create a simplified convex shape. This creates a slightly non-uniform wrong shape.
- Use `apply_force(Vector3.UP * 25)` on a script
- 
Result: Pushes object up without rotating it. Because center of mass is always at local 0,0,0

Change to JoltPhysics
- Run the same code again

Result: Pushes the object up with a bit of rotation (correct result for jolt)
Center of mass is auto-calculated for each shape and because of the non-uniform shape, it is **not** at 0,0,0.



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Jolt MRP, auto calculates shape's center of mass correctly: [physics_center_of_mass(jolt-works).zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19558745/physics_center_of_mass.jolt-works.zip)

GodotPhysics, doesn't auto calculate shape's center of mass, uses shape's origin: [physics_center_of_mass(godotphysics-dontwork).zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19558749/physics_center_of_mass.godotphysics-dontwork.zip)

summary: [macOS] Use productCategory instead of vendorName for joypad name

description.text:
The current macos joypad implementation uses `vendorName` as the joy name.

The `vendorName` on macos is just the local device name of the controller. This name can also be changed by the user by simply changing the name of the device in the Bluetooth settings. `productCategory` on the other hand always returns a more fitting unchanging device name.

The current implementation causes inconsistencies with other platforms, where `get_joy_name` just provides the name of the controller according to the specifications. If I connect my Switch Pro Controller that I renamed to "My amazing controller" to my laptop, vendorName is "My amazing controller", so `get_joy_name` returns "My amazing controller" whereas e.g. on Windows it returns "Nintendo Switch Pro Controller". If `get_joy_name` retrieved `productCategory` instead the result would be "Switch Pro Controller", which would help properly identify which controller is actually connected to the computer.

[Relevant Apple Docs](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/gamecontroller/gcdevice)

summary: Custom cursor reverts to default OS cursor in non-exclusive fullscreen when the MacOS dock is touched briefly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.2.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M3 Max - Apple M3 Max (16 threads)

### Issue description

On MacOS, in fullscreen (but not exclusive fullscreen), a custom cursor can be reliably and buggily disabled by briefly tapping the bottom of the screen where the MacOS Dock is. If this touch is brief enough that the MacOS Dock doesn't actually activate, then the custom cursor is reverted to the default OS cursor even after returning the cursor to the main part of the game window.

Video showing the issue: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uyTHHUHbNuM

The cursor can be returned to the correct, custom cursor by hovering over the Dock until it actually opens, hovering over the top bar of the screen, or switching windows. My theory is that Godot will fix the issue if the [`mouse_enter_window`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/platform/macos/display_server_macos.mm#L370) DisplayServer function is called.

This issue is similar to #76038 but probably not exactly the same. The symptoms in the repro videos look fairly different.

I found a couple of workarounds:

1. Use exclusive fullscreen mode instead of fullscreen.

2. Forcing the window to refocus periodically like this (could maybe do in `_process` too but it doesn't really need to be every frame):

```
func _reset_mouse_cursor() -> void:
	if get_window().has_focus():
		get_window().grab_focus()
	await get_tree().create_timer(0.1).timeout
	_reset_mouse_cursor()

func _ready():
	Input.set_custom_mouse_cursor(arrow_cursor, Input.CURSOR_ARROW)
	_reset_mouse_cursor()
```

I think the second workaround works because it causes DisplayServer's [`window_move_to_foreground`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/scene/main/window.cpp#L1997) to get called, which probably does something similar to switching screens back and forth.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open an empty project on MacOS.

2. Set a custom cursor:

```
var cursor = load("res://icon.svg")

func _ready() -> void:
Input.set_custom_mouse_cursor(cursor)
```

3. Run the project.

4. Go to fullscreen. (Note, NOT "exclusive fullscreen", where the bug doesn't seem to appear)

5. Brush the MacOS Dock at the bottom of the screen, and the custom cursor will revert to the default OS cursor even while hovering over the full-screened game.

6. The bug can be undone by making the game realize the window has been refocused in some way, e.g. by switching screens, hovering over the dock for longer and then going back, or hovering at the top of the screen and then going back.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/jasharpe/macos-cursor-repro

summary: Path3D/Curve3D & PathFollow3D incompletness/inconsistencies or bugs with Rotation Mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Ubuntu 24.10 Wayland- Vulkan 1.3.280 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060

### Issue description

I'm trying to get to the bottom of how `PathFollow3D` is supposed to work in the Godot devs' conception. So I don't know if this is a bug or an documentation issue.

---

Path3D node holds a Curve3D resource which has a property called `up_vector_enabled`. The docs say that:

```c
bool up_vector_enabled [default: true]
     set_up_vector_enabled(value) setter
     is_up_vector_enabled() getter
```
> If `true`, the curve will bake up vectors used for orientation. This is used when `PathFollow3D.rotation_mode` is set to `PathFollow3D.ROTATION_ORIENTED`. Changing it forces the cache to be recomputed.

Furthermore, the docs on `PathFollow3D.ROTATION_ORIENTED` is:

> Uses the up vector information in a `Curve3D` to enforce orientation. This rotation mode requires the `Path3D`'s `Curve3D.up_vector_enabled` property to be set to true.

And we get a warning on the `PathFollow3D` node when using `ROTATION_ORIENTED` and `Curve3D.up_vector_enabled` is set to `false`:

> PathFollow3D's ROTATION_ORIENTED requires "Up Vector" to be enabled in its parent Path3D's Curve resource.

---

Now this all makes me think that `ROTATION_ORIENTED` is supposed to orient the `PathFollow3D` UP vector with the Curve3D UP vector. But the source code implementation doesn't do any of that, instead when `PathFollow3D.ROTATION_ORIENTED` is used it orients the `PathFollow3D` UP vector with the world UP vector AKA `Vector3.UP`. See implementation at 

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/cc7217970d02b84122b59569cf7ed3b52cf815a6/scene/3d/path_3d.cpp#L373

Turns out that `PathFollow3D.ROTATION_XYZ` is the option that actually orients the `PathFollow3D` node UP vector with the curve UP vector (be it cached with `Curve3D.up_vector_enabled` or not).

This is all confusing and I'm even wondering if there's a need for `PathFollow3D.ROTATION_ORIENTED`. What would be the difference between `ROTATION_ORIENTED` and `ROTATION_XYZ`? Since they both use the `Curve3D.sample_baked_with_rotation()` transform. See implementation at 

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/cc7217970d02b84122b59569cf7ed3b52cf815a6/scene/3d/path_3d.cpp#L288-L288

### Steps to reproduce

I provided a MRP to further explore the problem.

Play with the *Path3D -> Curve3D -> Up Vector -> Enabled* and different values for *PathFollow3D -> Rotation Mode* options in the *Inspector* and check the UP vector of the `Marker3D` node in different positions of the curve.

Noticeably, when *Path3D -> Curve3D -> Up Vector -> Enabled* is checked and *PathFollow3D -> Rotation Mode -> Oriented* is selected you can see that the curve UP vector doesn't influence the `PathFollow3D` UP vector as the documents say between points at indices 1 & 2 of the `Curve3D`. See attached screenshot as example:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/41277e8e-124d-4940-a633-f4b5732e377c)

Whereas with *PathFollow3D -> Rotation Mode -> XYZ*, the `PathFollow3D` UP vector is aligned with the `Curve3D` up vector (with or without `Curve3D.up_vector_enabled` on). See attached screenshot:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9352bd10-d715-484f-bb7e-327bebac4e15)

So the question is, what's the intended difference between `ROTATION_ORIENTED` & `ROTATION_XYZ`? Is there even a need for `ROTATION_ORIENTED`?

[godot-dbg.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19549151/godot-dbg.zip)

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
- Not reproducible in: v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Debian GNU/Linux trixie/sid trixie on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7600 (RADV NAVI33) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

Under 4.4.1, (Noto Color) emojis are affected by a control's font color, even when that emoji already has its own colours:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4284981c-8af2-4936-9ff1-6da0bfc234a3)

This was not the case in 4.4:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/afdfdaa1-fdf8-4e36-831e-fa4fb2e11449)


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `Label`, `LineEdit`, or `Button`
2. Assign Noto Color Emoji as the font (via `LabelSettings` or theme override)
3. Set the visible text to include a coloured emoji
4. Change the font color (via `LabelSettings` or theme override) to something other than white
5. ⚠️👀

summary: The assembly not belonging to this project contains Godot types. When using this type as a parent class, the `This inspector might be out of date` warning still persists after compiling the project.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot_v4.4-stable_mono_win64
Godot_v4.5-dev1_mono_win64

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot_v4.4-stable_mono_win64 - Vulkan (Forward+)  NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 32.0.15.6636

### Issue description

I'm extracting some common functionalities to my new project and want to share these utility components. I created a C# class library project to store these tools, referencing the following packages from NuGet:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Godot.SourceGenerators" Version="4.4.1" />
<PackageReference Include="GodotSharp" Version="4.4.1" />
<PackageReference Include="GodotSharpEditor" Version="4.4.1" />
```

And configured this as a Godot tools project:
```xml
<IsGodotToolsProject>true</IsGodotToolsProject>
```

I then created a local NuGet package for sharing with other projects. However, when declaring Godot-related types (like using Node2D as a parent class) in my tools project, and having project scripts inherit through `MyNode2D -> NuGetAssemblyNode2D -> Node2D`, the implementation fails to work properly.

The Godot editor outputs these logs (reproduced with latest version):
```
Godot Engine v4.5.dev1.mono.official (c) 2007-present Juan Linietsky, Ariel Manzur & Godot Contributors.
--- Debug adapter server started on port 6006 ---
--- GDScript language server started on port 6005 ---
  ERROR: Resource file not found: res:// (expected type: Script)
```

Additionally, the inspector persistently shows `This inspector might be out of date. Please build the C# project`, and this warning never disappears regardless of how many times I rebuild the project.

### Steps to reproduce

The following steps can reproduce the same issue:

1. Create a new Godot project.  
2. Open Project Settings, check "Show Advanced Settings", set `.NET` > `dotnet/project/solution_directory` to `../`.  
3. Close Project Settings. Click the Project tab, select `Tools -> C# -> Create C# Solution`.  
4. Create a `Node2D` node and attach a script `SuperNode.cs`.  
5. Open the C# solution. Create a class library project named `VirtualNugetAssembly`, and install Godot NuGet packages:  
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Godot.SourceGenerators" Version="4.4.1" />
<PackageReference Include="GodotSharp" Version="4.4.1" />
<PackageReference Include="GodotSharpEditor" Version="4.4.1" />
```  
6. Configure the project as a tools project:  
```xml
<IsGodotToolsProject>true</IsGodotToolsProject>
```  
7. Create a `SuperNode2D` class in `VirtualNugetAssembly` inheriting from `Node2D`.  
8. Add an exported property:  
```csharp
[Export] public string? AiName { get; set; }
```  
9. In the Godot project's `SuperNode.cs`, change the inheritance from `Node2D` to `VirtualNugetAssembly.SuperNode2D`.  
10. Open the editor, click "Build" in the top-right corner.
